# agentic-misp-mcp

## Summary

agentic-misp-mcp is an MCP server for analyst-oriented MISP workflows. It is workflow-first, not
endpoint-first. It exposes 25 bounded tools for IOC search/investigation, pivoting, event
search/summarization, sightings, warninglist checks, read-only feed observability, report
generation, dry-run proposal generation, and tightly controlled approval-gated writes.

It must not become a raw MISP API proxy.

## Project status (v0.3.2)

- Current release: `v0.3.2`, a minimal date-validation hardening patch on `v0.3.1` based on
  external review of commit `681fe17`. `search_events` now rejects calendar-invalid dates (e.g.
  `2026-13-45`, `2026-02-30`) that previously passed shape-only regex validation. No MCP tool,
  scoring, write-surface, or approval-workflow changes relative to `v0.3.1`.
- Functional baseline: `v0.3.0` (2026-07-05) — age-aware scoring (freshness labels + weighted
  scores, default on), six new read-only tools (`get_ioc_sightings`, `search_events`,
  `get_misp_status`, `list_feeds`, `get_feed_status`, `summarize_feed_health`), and a read-tool
  response envelope (`tool_name`, `schema_version: 1`). All response changes were additive.
- Current MCP tool count: **25**. Do not document a higher count: a no-op feed-proposal tool
  found during the v0.3.0 review was removed before merge (`docs/review-v0.3.0-findings.md`,
  finding F2) and must not be reintroduced.
- Automated tests: 358 passing (mocked MISP responses).
- Live validation: MISP `2.5.42` lab, `v0.3.0` pass 14/14 checks —
  `docs/live-validation-report-v0.3.0.md`. Pre-merge review findings:
  `docs/review-v0.3.0-findings.md`. Earlier evidence (v0.2.x): the GA pass in
  `docs/live-validation-report-v0.2.0-rc.1.md`.
- Production claim scope: `v0.2.0` was declared GA for the MCP-server scope of this project
  only (server behavior, MISP API behavior, approval workflow, audit/redaction, config safety);
  `v0.3.x` extends that same scope. Not a SIEM/SOAR/SOC platform claim. Manual audit-log review
  is the accepted control, not automated alerting.
- Known limitations: only MISP `2.5.42` is validated (see `docs/misp-compatibility.md`); a live
  HTTP 429 has mocked coverage only; container/dependency/secret scanning and signed release
  artifacts are not yet part of CI/release; HTTP transport is experimental.
- Primary transport: stdio. Python 3.11+. License: MIT.

## Tool groups (25 total)

Investigation and read tools (read-only):

- search_ioc
- investigate_ioc
- pivot_ioc
- find_related_iocs
- summarize_event
- explain_event_context
- extract_event_iocs
- find_events_by_tag
- search_events
- get_ioc_sightings
- check_warninglists
- get_misp_status

Feed observability (read-only; responses redact URLs userinfo/sensitive query keys and
header/token-like fields):

- list_feeds
- get_feed_status
- summarize_feed_health

Reporting (read-only):

- generate_ioc_report
- generate_event_report
- generate_markdown_ioc_report
- generate_markdown_event_report

Proposal-only tools (dry-run; build/validate payloads, never call MISP write endpoints):

- propose_event
- propose_attribute

Approval-gated write tools:

- submit_ioc_with_approval
- add_sighting_with_approval
- tag_event_with_approval
- publish_event_with_approval

Write tools return explicit outcomes: blocked, invalid, pending_approval, executed, or failed.
"failed" means MISP was reached and did not raise an error, but MISP itself rejected the
operation (for example HTTP 200 with saved/published: false) — distinct from "executed", which
means MISP confirmed the write.

## Safety boundaries

Agents and contributors must preserve these boundaries:

- Do not expose raw MISP API passthrough tools.
- Do not expose feed fetch/cache/enable/disable/edit/delete tools or raw feed proxy behavior.
  Feed observability is read-only.
- Do not add generic admin tools for users, organisations, servers, settings, or unrestricted
  MISP endpoints.
- Do not expose the approval store to the agent: no MCP tool may create, approve, reject, or
  read approval records. Production approvals happen only via the operator CLI.
- Do not pass API keys, approval tokens, authorization headers, passwords, cookies, or secrets
  through MCP tool arguments.
- Do not add shell execution or unrestricted filesystem access.
- Do not add hidden mutation to read tools; read tools call read endpoints only.
- Keep propose_* tools dry-run/proposal-only.
- Keep read-only mode as the safe default.
- Keep controlled writes disabled by default with AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ENABLE_WRITE=false.
- Keep write approval required by default with AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=true.
- Preserve audit logging for every MCP tool call.
- Preserve argument sanitization and secret redaction.
- Preserve bounded output and response-size limits.

## Age-aware scoring (v0.3.0+)

- investigate_ioc / generate_ioc_report / pivot_ioc responses include a `freshness` block with
  labels fresh / aging / stale / expired / unknown (default thresholds 30/90/365 days).
- With AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_AGE_WEIGHTING=true (default), positive score factors are discounted by
  intel age (default weights 1.0/0.75/0.4/0.15). Penalties (warninglist hits, benign tags) are
  never age-discounted. Expired-only intel is capped below the likely_malicious threshold and
  needs fresh corroboration to cross it.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_AGE_WEIGHTING=false reproduces v0.2.x scoring exactly; the freshness block
  is emitted either way.

## Important environment variables

- MISP_URL: required MISP base URL.
- MISP_API_KEY: required runtime API key; never pass as a tool argument.
- MISP_VERIFY_TLS: default true; false is lab-only for self-signed certificates.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ROLE: read_only, analyst_write, curator, or admin.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ENABLE_WRITE: default false.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_REQUIRE_APPROVAL: default true.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_APPROVAL_MODE: lab (default) or production.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_APPROVAL_TOKEN: optional lab/shared-secret hardening only; audit logs redact
  it. It is not the production approval mechanism.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_APPROVAL_STORE_PATH: SQLite production approval store; agent must not have
  write access.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_AUDIT_LOG_PATH: JSONL audit path.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES: maximum MISP response size before JSON parsing
  (fail-closed).
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_AGE_WEIGHTING / AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_AGE_WEIGHTS /
  AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_FRESHNESS_{FRESH,AGING,STALE}_DAYS: age-aware scoring controls.
- AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_FEED_{FRESH,STALE}_DAYS: feed health thresholds (defaults 7/30).

## Audit semantics

Audit logs are JSONL.

Expected audit outcomes:

- Allowed successful call: success=true, outcome=success.
- Blocked policy decision: allowed=false, success=false, outcome=blocked.
- Runtime error or validation failure: success=false, outcome=error.
- Invalid proposal/write payload (tool status="invalid"): outcome=invalid.
- Controlled write reached MISP but MISP rejected the operation (tool status="failed"):
  success=false, outcome=failed. Distinct from both blocked and error.

Sensitive values such as approval_token, API keys, authorization headers, bearer tokens,
cookies, passwords, and secrets must be redacted.

## Production approval mode

- Lab mode (AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_APPROVAL_MODE=lab) is the default. approved=true is a lab
  programmatic gate; AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_APPROVAL_TOKEN is only optional lab/shared-secret
  hardening.
- Production mode requires AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_APPROVAL_MODE=production; approved=true alone never
  executes writes, even if AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false.
- Production writes require an operator-approved approval_request_id created in SQLite and
  approved via `agentic-misp-mcp approvals ...`; approvals are one-time-use, TTL-bound, and
  exact operation-hash-bound.
- The LLM/agent must not have shell access to the approval CLI or write access to the approval
  database. If redemption succeeds but MISP later fails, the approval is consumed and retry
  needs a new operator approval.
- Guardrails: AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTE_TYPES,
  AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTE_CATEGORIES, AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ALLOWED_TAGS,
  AGENTIC_MISP_MCP_ENABLE_PUBLISH (default false).

## Operator CLI (not MCP tools)

- `agentic-misp-mcp config-check`: validates configuration without contacting MISP; redacts the
  API key.
- `agentic-misp-mcp config doctor`: deeper PASS/WARN/FAIL operational-readiness checks
  (write/approval-mode pairing, publish/role pairing, approval-store and audit-log permission
  safety, allowlist coverage, TTL length, temp paths). Never connects to MISP, never prints
  secrets, exits nonzero on any FAIL.
- `agentic-misp-mcp approvals ...`: production approval management (list/approve/reject/prune).
  `approvals prune --older-than <duration> [--vacuum]` deletes only terminal records.

## Docker usage

Build:

```bash
cd /path/to/agentic-misp-mcp
docker build -t agentic-misp-mcp:local .
```

Run config-check:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  --env-file /home/user/.config/agentic-misp-mcp/.env \
  -v /home/user/.local/state/agentic-misp-mcp/logs:/app/logs \
  agentic-misp-mcp:local \
  config-check
```

Run stdio server:

```bash
docker run --rm -i \
  --env-file /home/user/.config/agentic-misp-mcp/.env \
  -v /home/user/.local/state/agentic-misp-mcp/logs:/app/logs \
  agentic-misp-mcp:local \
  --transport stdio
```

Run with MCP Inspector:

```bash
npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector \
  docker run --rm -i \
    --env-file /home/user/.config/agentic-misp-mcp/.env \
    -v /home/user/.local/state/agentic-misp-mcp/logs:/app/logs \
    agentic-misp-mcp:local \
    --transport stdio
```

For headless hosts, forward Inspector ports:

```bash
ssh -L 6274:127.0.0.1:6274 -L 6277:127.0.0.1:6277 user@mcp-host.example.local
```

## Development commands

Use these commands before committing code changes:

```bash
uv run --extra dev ruff check .
uv run --extra dev ruff format --check .
uv run --extra dev pytest -q
```

Equivalent Make targets:

```bash
make lint
make format-check
make test
make check
```

## Known limitation

Some lab validation used historical OSINT data. Treat live-validation hits as compatibility and
workflow evidence, not current threat activity. Age-aware scoring lowers confidence for old
intel by default, but analyst workflows should still correlate with current local telemetry
before blocking or escalation.

## Instructions for coding agents

When editing this repository:

- Keep the server workflow-oriented.
- Preserve read-only default behavior.
- Preserve policy gates, approval gates, audit logging, and redaction.
- Add or update tests for any behavior change.
- Do not weaken role checks.
- Do not add raw MISP proxy functionality or feed mutation tools.
- Do not include secrets or private lab details in docs or tests.
- Prefer generic examples in documentation (`/path/to/agentic-misp-mcp`, `/home/user/...`,
  `/opt/...`).
- Keep the documented tool count at 25 unless the registry actually changes.
- Show diffs before committing. Commits are attributed to their human author only — no AI
  co-author trailers.
- Do not overstate production readiness. See `docs/production-readiness.md` for the current
  scope and acceptance criteria before making any production-status claim, and
  `docs/misp-compatibility.md` before claiming MISP version support.
